title: 23andMe: How the DNA Pioneer Took On the FDA and Redefined Health Tech | Sequoia Capital
excerpts:
Section Title: 23andMe: How the DNA Pioneer Took On the FDA and Redefined Health Tech > The People > Anne Wojcicki
Content:
Anne Wojcicki is the founder and CEO of 23andMe. She also cofounded the Breakthrough Prize, where she sits on the board. Previously she spent a decade investing in biotech companies.
 ... 
Section Title: 23andMe: How the DNA Pioneer Took On the FDA and Redefined Health Tech > Transcript
Content:
In today’s episode, we’ll hear how 23andMe confronted a regulator with the power to shut down the company, took a leap into the high-risk, high-reward territory of drug development, and transformed its consumer business after booming sales suddenly flatlined.
**Anne Wojcicki:** My name is Anne Wojcicki and I am the cofounder and CEO of 23andMe.

title: 23andMe ft. Anne Wojcicki - Ho… - Crucible Moments - Apple Podcasts
excerpts:
Section Title: 23andMe ft. Anne Wojcicki - How a DNA startup took on the FDA and redefined health tech
Content:
[Crucible Moments](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/crucible-moments/id1705282398)
Play
Millions of customers have explored their genome with 23andMe. But when the company started in 2006, the idea of consumer DNA testing was heresy to the medical establishment. The FDA once even ordered 23andMe to stop selling its health testing product. The company persevered to make allies out of adversaries, and became the only FDA-approved product on the market. Learn how 23andMe defined the DNA testing category, and used its success to enter the massive new field of drug discovery.
Host: Roelof Botha, Sequoia Capital
Featuring: Anne Wojcicki, Richard Scheller
Transcript: https://www.sequoiacap.com/podcast/crucible-moments-23andMe/

title: 23andMe (and You, and Everyone Else)
excerpts:
Section Title: 23andMe (and You, and Everyone Else) > Episode Transcript
Content:
Anne Wojcicki is C.E.O. and a co-founder of the personal-genetics-testing company 23andMe. It provides, for a fee of $99, a wealth of information about your ancestry, and for $199, an additional report on your health characteristics and risks. The company has about 10 million total customers, slightly more than half of whom buy the full ancestry-plus-health report. But those are one-time fees; is that enough to justify the company’s valuation of several billion dollars?

title: CEO Series: 23andMe's Anne Woj… ‑ HBR IdeaCast ‑ Apple Podcasts
excerpts:
Section Title: CEO Series: 23andMe's Anne Wojcicki on Scientific Breakthroughs and Public Trust
Content:
[HBR IdeaCast](https://podcasts.apple.com/fr/podcast/hbr-ideacast/id152022135)
Lire
Anne Wojcicki, CEO of 23andMe, spent a decade in healthcare and biotechnology before launching the DNA testing and analysis company in 2006. Her goal was twofold: to help individuals learn more about their own genetics, enabling them to pursue more personalized medical care, and to create a database of genetic information for commercial and academic researchers to promote broader improvements to the healthcare system. She speaks with HBR's Editor-in-Chief Adi Ignatius about tackling challenges in an emerging industry.
[Page Web de l'épisode](https://hbr.org/podcast/2021/05/ceo-series-23andmes-anne-wojcicki-on-scientific-breakthroughs-and-public-trust)

When Silicon Valley power-player Anne Wojcicki sees gold in genetics, she forms 23andMe. She wants to bring genetic testing to the masses. But behind the hype and celebrity endorsements, can she build a company that’s selling more than just an expensive novelty product?
